items.title: Alan Greenspan, Fed chair under 4 U.S. presidents, dies at age 100
items.standfirst: Alan Greenspan's lengthy reign at the Federal Reserve coincided with a period of stability from the mid-1980s until 2007.
items.content: Alan Greenspan's lengthy reign at the Federal Reserve coincided with a period of stability from the mid-1980s until 2007.

items.title: Former US Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan dies at age 100
items.standfirst: Greenspan presided over economic turbulence, including crises and booms, during his 18-year tenure as Fed chairman.
items.content: Greenspan presided over economic turbulence, including crises and booms, during his 18-year tenure as Fed chairman.

items.title: Former US Federal Reserve chairman Alan Greenspan dies at 100
items.standfirst: Alan Greenspan – the longtime US Federal Reserve chief who served under four presidents – has died at 100, his wife said in a statement on Monday. Greenspan presided over an unprecedented period of American economic expansion but was later faulted for failing to rein in markets ahead of the 2008 global financial crisis.
items.content: Alan Greenspan – the longtime US Federal Reserve chief who served under four presidents – has died at 100, his wife said in a statement on Monday. Greenspan presided over an unprecedented period of American economic expansion but was later faulted for failing to rein in markets ahead of the 2008 global financial crisis.

items.title: Alan Greenspan, longtime head of the US Federal Reserve, dies aged 100

items.content: Alan Greenspan, the longtime head of the Federal Reserve and influential economist, has died aged 100.

items.title: Alan Greenspan, architect of the modern American economy, dies aged 100
items.standfirst: As chairman of the Federal Reserve, Alan Greenspan became the world's most high-profile banker.
items.content: As chairman of the Federal Reserve, Alan Greenspan became the world's most high-profile banker.

items.title: Alan Greenspan, the legendary former Federal Reserve chair, dies
items.standfirst: During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.
items.content: During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.(Image credit: Paul J. Richards)

items.title: Alan Greenspan dies aged 100
items.standfirst: Longstanding Federal Reserve chair was lionised in office — but his legacy was reassessed in the financial crisis
items.content: Longstanding Federal Reserve chair was lionised in office — but his legacy was reassessed in the financial crisis

clusters.title: Trump-backed candidate wins Colombian election
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump backed candidate wins Colombian election

items.title: Trump-backed candidate wins Colombian election
items.standfirst: A fiercely divided Colombia has chosen a new president in a razor-thin runoff that’s already sparking tension across the country. While some celebrate the victory of right-wing candidate Abelardo de la Espriella, others are protesting what they see as a dangerous shift to the right.
items.content: A fiercely divided Colombia has chosen a new president in a razor-thin runoff that’s already sparking tension across the country. While some celebrate the victory of right-wing candidate Abelardo de la Espriella, others are protesting what they see as a dangerous shift to the right.

items.title: Far-right de la Espriella elected Colombia president: What’s next?
items.standfirst: Election results show Trump-endorsed millionaire Abelardo de la Espriella wins by razor-thin margin.
items.content: Election results show Trump-endorsed millionaire Abelardo de la Espriella wins by razor-thin margin.

items.title: Far-right millionaire wins Colombia’s razor-tight presidential election
items.standfirst: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majorityA Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” has defeated a leftwing senator by a razor-thin margin to win Colombia’s presidential runoff, in an election that promises to mark a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict.With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, the far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella had secured 12.96m votes, or 49.66%, just 250,830 more than the leftist senator Iván Cepeda, who received 12.7m votes, or 48.7%. A further 1.6% of ballots were cast blank. Continue reading...
items.content: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majorityA Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” has defeated a leftwing senator by a razor-thin margin to win Colombia’s presidential runoff, in an election that promises to mark a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict.With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, the far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella had secured 12.96m votes, or 49.66%, just 250,830 more than the leftist senator Iván Cepeda, who received 12.7m votes, or 48.7%. A further 1.6% of ballots were cast blank. Continue reading...

items.title: Colombians vote in runoff election expected to trigger shift in decades-long armed conflict
items.standfirst: Frontrunner Abelardo de la Espriella has vowed to return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groupsGhost of far-right paramilitaries hovers over Colombia’s presidential runoff voteColombians are going to the polls in a presidential runoff expected to trigger a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict, now at its most violent point since the landmark 2016 peace agreement between the government and most of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc).Polls show the frontrunner is the Trump-admiring far-right lawyer and millionaire businessman Abelardo de la Espriella, who has vowed to abandon President Gustavo Petro’s “total peace” plan of negotiating the disarmament of all criminal organisations and instead return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups. Continue reading...
items.content: Frontrunner Abelardo de la Espriella has vowed to return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groupsGhost of far-right paramilitaries hovers over Colombia’s presidential runoff voteColombians are going to the polls in a presidential runoff expected to trigger a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict, now at its most violent point since the landmark 2016 peace agreement between the government and most of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc).Polls show the frontrunner is the Trump-admiring far-right lawyer and millionaire businessman Abelardo de la Espriella, who has vowed to abandon President Gustavo Petro’s “total peace” plan of negotiating the disarmament of all criminal organisations and instead return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Abelardo de la Espriella wins Colombian presidential election
Election results show razor thin margin of victory
Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities
De la Espriella secures 49.66 percent of votes
Election promises to mark dramatic shift in armed conflict
